timesoft
  • »
  • Level 2: Maps and Linemaps
  • View page source

Level 2: Maps and Linemaps¶

The level 2 data products of TIME observations are maps and, for one-dimensional scan patterns, “line maps” of data binned along the scan axis. These data can be generated by the timesoft.Timestream class and, like the level 1 timestream data are saved in zipped numpy file archives (.npz). They can be loaded, analyzed, and saved using methods of the timesoft.Map class (2D data) and timesoft.LineMap class (1D data).

Contents of the Map and LineMap Files¶

The map .npz files contain a number of elements.

Data Element

Data Type

Description

header

dictionary

Summary information about the file/observation

x_edge

1D array

The edges of map pixels along the x-axis

y_edge

1D array

The edges of map pixels along the y-axis

maps

3D array

An n_detectors x map_y_size x map_x_size array of maps

counts

2D array

The number of timestream samples averaged in each pixel

extras

dictionary

A dictionary containing additional 2D arrays

The line map .npz files contain a similar elements, but with reduced dimensions

Data Element

Data Type

Description

header

dictionary

Summary information about the file/observation

ax_edge

1D array

The edges of the bins along the scan axis

linemaps

2D array

An n_detectors x n_bins array of binned data

counts

1D array

The number of timestream samples averaged in each bin

extras

dictionary

A dictionary containing additional 1D arrays

Headers¶

Most items in the headers of level 1, level 2 and level 3 data (timestreams, maps, and power spectra) are the same, and simply inherited from the lower level data products. See the section on contents of the timestream headers for information on these basic contents.

Any given file may also have arbitrary items added to the header (which will in turn be inherited by derived data products). These can be used for exptending the functionality of the data analysis pipeline, or simply for adding notes to a given piece of data.

Maps and LineMaps each have one unique piece of header information that is not associated with their parent timestream.

Additional Map Header Entry ‘map_pars’: This header key references another dictionary containing information about the timesoft.Map object. It includes pixel size, map center, and map dimensions.

Additional LineMap Header Entry ‘1d_pars’: This header key references another dictionary containing information about the timesoft.LineMap object. It includes bin size, scan center, and scan length.

The ‘Extras’ Attribute¶

The .extras attribute of a timesoft.Map or timesoft.LineMap instance is a dictionary, which can be used to store additional data, binned on the same grid as the map or linemap data. This can be used to things like the average elevation of samples falling in given pixel/bin.

Maps of arbitrary properties can be stored in the extras dictionary, and should be assoicated with a descriptive key.

Note

This feature is currently only implemented for the timesoft.LineMap class.
